raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The reintroduced legislation will prohibit the State Lands Commission from approving new leases for pipelines, piers, wharves, or other infrastructure needed to support new federal oil and gas development in California's three-mile area.",
    "The legislation would prohibit any lease renewal, extension, or modification that would support the production, transportation, or processing of new oil and gas.",
    "The proposed bill aims to protect the health of residents living and working near the coast and to help prevent future oil spills.",
    "California's coastal economy produces approximately $44.5 billion in GDP each year.",
    "California's coastal economy employs almost half a million people in the state.",
    "The California Coastal Sanctuary Act passed in 1994 prohibits new oil and gas leases in the state’s coastal waters, with some exceptions."
  ]
}
```
